All right, now we are taking a look at the Challenger Group tracking sheets. So whenever you guys are signing people up into your challenge and they have just been through the sales presentation and orientation and they've signed up, we need to put them on the Challenger tracking sheet. So the clipboard system is what you're using to track them. It's a client facing system and you're tracking their weight week to week and making sure that they are getting results. This is going to be the end week or if you start a new group every two weeks, just make sure you can name your groups whatever you want. This is just how to keep track of them. So this would be an example of a group that we started in May. So it's got their first name, their last name, their email, their phone number and then we click and highlight what kind of Challenger they are. So are they doing a classic challenge, six week challenge at your gym? Are they doing the at home challenge? You did a no sale sale and they are doing the at home or are they doing an online challenge? So a classic challenge can be a six week or 42 day, a 12 week HTTP. It can be whatever you want but this is at the gym. These other two are not at the gym and then online is going to be the same rate or the same price is at the gym but they're just doing online one on one coaching with you. So that is going to be the different options. One you want to put down what weigh in day they have. So this is all being done before their weigh in day. This is when you just made the sale. So what is their weigh in day? Then we have another box of if they showed or not. Then moving farther right we have their halfway meeting date. When are they just do that or when you print it off just type in how many visits they had each one of these weeks and this is something your challenge manager or accountability coach or your owner, whoever you decide to assign this to this is going to be a huge indicator if they are going to continue or not. So you guys should be reaching out to them if they have zero visits or less than three visits per week based on our contracts and based on our fulfillment of the challenge. They're supposed to be coming in three times per week. So if they're not this is where we're going to find that out and reach out to them and we'll go over that more in the fulfillment area of the challenge but this is where we're actually tracking the numbers. Then as soon as you know if they are continuing or not you want to check this box because that's going to make it so you don't have to continue working them as hard to get them to continue. So you can just check these boxes and make sure you check all these because it's going to dynamically update other sheets down here. So as soon as you know they're continuing maybe they signed their next agreement or they bought a year or whatever it may be check that off. Moving farther right we want to see how many of them bought supplements so you want to check off any one of these that purchase supplements on way in day or throughout the challenge whether your challenge coach is recommending supplements because they're not maybe getting the best results. This is where you check off if they purchase them. Lastly you want to check off this should be halfway. So you want to check off if they took the offer at the halfway point. So this is just an indicator for yourself whether you have to pitch them at way out or not. So if they took the offer at the halfway point you don't have to pitch them on way out day. You still want to do way out day because they're finishing their challenge but it's a reminder to yourself that you do not have to pitch them. If they did not take the offer on the halfway and you go over here and take a look and it says they showed up for their halfway meeting over here then you know that you did pitch them and they've heard about it so you need to maybe come at them with a different angle or a reminder or look in your notes and see why they didn't take the offer but you still want to then pitch them again on way out day. Then the final thing that we're tracking on this sheet is going to be if they purchased a paid-in full or if they purchased an EFT to continue. So this is giving you all the details of all of your challengers. It'll help keep you very organized. And anytime you start a new group all you're going to do is click here on the master group that is blank and you are going to click duplicate. And then you are going to click on rename and you can name this let's just say May 3. And now May 3 is open and you are going to start filling it out with names when they're weighing in and all of that stuff. So that is what these two first sheets are the blank one and then an example of one when it's filled out and what it looks like and this will make it very, very organized. So now we're going to move one farther right into the tab that says list of all challengers. So this is going to be exactly how it sounds just a list of all of your challengers and we want to keep this updated because this is where it's going to collect some of our stats and some of our data for us on exactly what's happening at your gym with all of your groups combined. So now that you added them from here you added everybody into your group here all you have to do is copy them. And we can go to list of all challengers and we can come over here and we can paste them. Cool. So now we have all of our challengers from our other sheet and we just added them to this sheet. Now all we have to do is mark if they are doing a classic challenge if they are doing a at home challenge right or an online challenge. So that is what you're going to do because when we go over to your challenge group summaries if you were to put in whoops before I move on to that let me just fully explain this is very very simple that's all that's on this sheet is just a list of all of your challengers ongoing right as you put the challenge group over here in this area it's going to give you those as an option on your master. So we have one person in there and you can see this is this is just a classic challengers and it says one here.
